Gosper, C.R., Coates, D.J., Hopper, S.D., Byrne, M. & Yates, C.J. (2020)

threatened flora in the southwest australian floristic
region](https://doi.org/10.1093/biolinnean/blaa141). *Biological Journal
of the Linnean Society*, **133**, 394--410.

Kougioumoutzis, K., Kokkoris, I.P., Panitsa, M., Trigas, P., Strid, A. &
Dimopoulos, P. (2020) [Spatial phylogenetics, biogeographical patterns
and conservation implications of the endemic flora of crete (aegean,
greece) under climate change
scenarios](https://doi.org/10.3390/biology9080199). *Biology*, **9**,
199.

Mishler, B.D., Guralnick, R., Soltis, P.S., Smith, S.A., Soltis, D.E.,
Barve, N., Allen, J.M. & Laffan, S.W. (2020) [Spatial phylogenetics of
the north american flora](https://doi.org/10.1111/jse.12590). *Journal
of Systematics and Evolution*, **58**, 393--405.

Moles, A.T., Laffan, S.W., Keighery, M., Dalrymple, R.L., Tindall, M.L.
& Chen, S. (2020) [A hairy situation: Plant species in warm, sunny
places are more likely to have pubescent
leaves](https://doi.org/10.1111/jbi.13870). *Journal of Biogeography*,
**47**, 1934--1944.

Moraes, A.M., Milward-de-Azevedo, M.A., Menini Neto, L. & Faria, A.P.G.
de (2020) [Distribution patterns of passiflora l. (Passifloraceae s.s.)
in the serra da mantiqueira, southeast
brazil](https://doi.org/10.1007/s40415-020-00665-w). *Brazilian Journal
of Botany*, **43**, 999--1012.

Pereira, L.C., Chautems, A. & Menini Neto, L. (2020) [Biogeography and
conservation of gesneriaceae in the serra da mantiqueira, southeastern
region of brazil](https://doi.org/10.1007/s40415-020-00671-y).

[Remote sensing of plant
biodiversity](https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-030-33157-3) (2020) Springer
International Publishing.

Sosa, V., Vásquez-Cruz, M. & Villarreal-Quintanilla, J.A. (2020)
[Influence of climate stability on endemism of the vascular plants of
the chihuahuan desert](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jaridenv.2020.104139).
*Journal of Arid Environments*, **177**, 104139.

Suissa, J.S. & Sundue, M.A. (2020) [Diversity patterns of neotropical
ferns: Revisiting tryon's centers of richness and
endemism](https://doi.org/10.1640/0002-8444-110.4.211). *American Fern
Journal*, **110**.

Toro‐Núñez, O. & Lira‐Noriega, A. (2020) [Discordant phylogenetic
endemism patterns in a recently diversified brassicaceae lineage from
the atacama desert: When choices in phylogenetics and species
distribution information matter](https://doi.org/10.1111/jbi.13846).
*Journal of Biogeography*, **47**, 1792--1804.

Xu, M., Yang, L., Kong, H., Wen, F. & Kang, M. (2020) [Congruent spatial
patterns of species richness and phylogenetic diversity in karst flora:

(gesneriaceae)](https://doi.org/10.1111/jse.12558). *Journal of

**11**, 57--70.

and terrestrial hotspots of evolutionary

there a latitudinal gradient in the proportion of species with
spinescence?](https://doi.org/10.1093/jpe/rtw031) *Journal of Plant

(2016) [Conservation status and threats for african

T.E., Hemmings, F.A., Tindall, M.L. & Moles, A.T. (2015) [Birds,
butterflies and flowers in the tropics are not more colourful than those
at higher latitudes](https://doi.org/10.1111/geb.12368). *Global Ecology
and Biogeography*, **24**, 1424--1432.

Daru, B.H. & Roux, P.C. le (2015) [Marine protected areas are
insufficient to conserve global marine plant
diversity](https://doi.org/10.1111/geb.12412). *Global Ecology and
Biogeography*, **25**, 324--334.

[A revised area taxonomy of phytogeographical regions within the
australian bioregionalisation
atlas](https://doi.org/10.11646/phytotaxa.208.4.2). *Phytotaxa*,
**208**, 261--277.

González‐Orozco, C.E., Mishler, B.D., Miller, J.T., Laffan, S.W., Knerr,
N., Unmack, P., Georges, A., Thornhill, A.H., Rosauer, D.F. & Gruber, B.
(2015) [Assessing biodiversity and endemism using phylogenetic methods
across multiple taxonomic groups](https://doi.org/10.1002/ece3.1747).
*Ecology and Evolution*, **5**, 5177--5192.

australian example](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.scitotenv.2015.04.113).

and geochemistry in

for understanding evolutionary
histories](https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1365-2699.2004.01127.x). *Journal

Laffan, S.W. & Crisp, M.D. (2003) [Assessing endemism at multiple
spatial scales, with an example from the australian vascular
